Chaz Lanier still stuck on 17 points.

items.title: Onyenso said he was having conversations about being the No. 60 pick (or undrafted) with his agent before the Pistons surprised him by moving to get him at No. 53. "It was an amazing experience for me. It's one-in-a-lifetime stuff. I'm really glad to be here."
items.author: Omari Sankofa II
items.body_excerpt: Onyenso said he was having conversations about being the No. 60 pick (or undrafted) with his agent before the Pistons surprised him by moving to get him at No. 53. "It was an amazing experience for me. It's one-in-a-lifetime stuff. I'm really glad to be here."

items.title: Ugonna Onyenso: “I’m a defensive-minded person. When I block shots, it’s fun for me to do that.” Said being on a defensive team like the Pistons suits him.

media.title: Pistons trade for No. 53 pick Ugonna Onyenso after Isaiah Stewart trade
media.description: Virginia center Ugonna Onyenso, 21, averaged 6.5 points, 4.9 rebounds and 2.9 blocks in just 18.6 minutes per game last season, joins Detroit Pistons.

items.title: The Detroit Pistons will now be hard-capped at the second apron after using cash in the trade to acquire Ugonna Onyenso.
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
The Detroit Pistons will now be hard-capped at the second apron after using cash in the trade to acquire Ugonna Onyenso.

Won't matter as Detroit is going to be pretty far from the second apron.

items.title: Sources: The Knicks traded No. 53 Ugonna Onyenso to the Detroit Pistons for cash.

items.body_excerpt: Sources: The Knicks traded No. 53 Ugonna Onyenso to the Detroit Pistons for cash.

items.title: Knicks take Ugonna Onyenso, a center, at No. 53. Played for three colleges and averaged, at least, 2.8 blocks per game in two of his college seasons.
items.author: James L. Edwards III
items.body_excerpt:
Knicks take Ugonna Onyenso, a center, at No. 53. Played for three colleges and averaged, at least, 2.8 blocks per game in two of his college seasons.

Maybe best shot blocker in draft.

Assuming NY is done, I'll have a story tomorrow AM on what the draft means for the Knicks' big picture.

items.title: TRADE: The New York Knicks have selected Ugonna Onyenso with the 53rd pick in the 2026 NBA Draft and he will be traded to the Detroit Pistons in exchange for cash considerations, league sources say.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: TRADE: The New York Knicks have selected Ugonna Onyenso with the 53rd pick in the 2026 NBA Draft and he will be traded to the Detroit Pistons in exchange for cash considerations, league sources say.

items.title: I'm not a lip reader, but Ugonna Onyenso's reaction seems to support my initial read that he said "what the fuck" after he was picked
items.author: Dan Devine
items.body_excerpt: I'm not a lip reader, but Ugonna Onyenso's reaction seems to support my initial read that he said "what the fuck" after he was picked

items.title: FWIW -- I wouldn't mind seeing Atlanta trade a future second rounder for Ugonna Onyenso.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
FWIW -- I wouldn't mind seeing Atlanta trade a future second rounder for Ugonna Onyenso.  

Think he's probably the best rim protector in the entire draft other than Mara.

Obviously real limitations elsewhere, but can see him being a backup center in the NBA for a long time.
